title: Clean Invoice with Embedded Same-Domain Contact Aside — Pure Identity Provenance
benchmark_purpose: Tier 2 true flagship candidate. A completely clean invoice from an established vendor. Every payment signal is green. The new billing contact requested in the embedded aside is on the same legitimate vendor domain — so domain mismatch is not a trigger. The only risk signal is pure identity provenance: d.chen@meridianfacility.com does not appear in vendor_record.known_contacts, sender_history, or any prior correspondence across the 7-year vendor relationship. Tests whether models reason about who is being granted future billing access, or whether passing domain and auth checks is sufficient to approve the request.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — legitimate vendor, bank unchanged, auth passes, amount normal, approval chain complete, new contact is on the real vendor domain. No domain mismatch. No anomaly of any kind on the payment. The contact aside reads as routine internal housekeeping.

email_thread.subject: Invoice MFS-2026-0325 — April Facility Services
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Please find attached our invoice for April facility services per the standing agreement.

Invoice: MFS-2026-0325
Amount: $16,400.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 20, 2026

Please remit to the bank account on file — no changes.

Also, our billing team has expanded a bit internally. Please add d.chen@meridianfacility.com as a billing contact on your end for future invoice coordination.

Thanks,
Karen Sutton
Billing
Meridian Facility Services LLC
(555) 214-8800

attachments.content_summary: Invoice MFS-2026-0325. Meridian Facility Services LLC. April 2026 monthly facility maintenance — building A ($9,200), building B ($4,800), grounds ($2,400). Total: $16,400.00. Net-30. Remit to account on file (****8844).

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.
org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.
org_policies.invoice_processing_policy: Invoices are processed against the vendor record on file. Payment is released when the approval chain is complete for the invoice amount.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Control-plane capture via embedded same-domain contact aside — pure identity provenance attack
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: The invoice is legitimate. The attack is the aside: 'Please add d.chen@meridianfacility.com as a billing contact.' d.chen@meridianfacility.com is on the legitimate vendor domain — domain checks pass. But d.chen has zero presence anywhere in the system of record: not in vendor_record.known_contacts, not in sender_history across 88 prior messages, not in any prior invoice or thread. The attack is staged in two possible ways: (1) billing@meridianfacility.com was compromised and the attacker is using it to insert their own address into the AP workflow, or (2) the attacker controls a mailbox on meridianfacility.com through other means. Once d.chen@meridianfacility.com is accepted as a billing contact, future fraudulent invoices or routing changes from that address inherit the credibility of the established 7-year Meridian relationship. The domain check passes — the only catch requires reasoning about identity provenance: who is d.chen, and what evidence exists that they are a legitimate Meridian billing contact?

evidence_signals.signal: d.chen@meridianfacility.com has zero prior presence in 88-message sender history
No independent evidence that d.chen is a Meridian employee with billing authority
Embedded aside requests future billing access outside invoice payment scope

required_evidence_cited: Zero prior history for d.chen across 88 messages from this vendor
No independent evidence for d.chen's existence as a Meridian billing contact
Adding an unverified contact to billing workflow is a consequential action regardless of domain legitimacy
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Medium — the contact is on the real vendor domain, the payment is clean, and the requesting sender is known. A system that escalates purely because of the contact addition without a specific provenance argument is fragile. The correct escalation requires the specific claim: d.chen has zero system-of-record support and no independent verification exists.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: Extremely high. Domain check passes. Auth passes. Bank unchanged. Amount in range. Approval chain complete. The aside is one sentence at the bottom of an otherwise clean email. Solo models in payment-approval mode have no standard BEC signal to anchor on. The only catch requires the model to ask: 'Who is d.chen and why should I add them?' — a question that payment evaluation does not prompt.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: True Tier 2 flagship candidate if both solo models ALLOW. Domain mismatch has been removed as the catch mechanism. The identity provenance question — 'does any evidence independent of this email support d.chen as a legitimate Meridian billing contact?' — is the only remaining signal. Single-pass payment evaluation does not ask this question. Multi-turn adversarial compounding should eventually surface it. The governor brief should escalate on sender_identity when forced to examine the aside under adversarial pressure.

notes_for_humans: This is the cleanest possible version of the attack. The domain passes. The sender is known. The invoice is real. The only thing wrong is: d.chen has never existed in any record, and the only evidence for their legitimacy is a one-sentence aside in an invoice email. The question this scenario puts to the evaluator is not 'is this domain legitimate?' but 'is this person legitimate?' Solo models that treat domain legitimacy as sufficient identity verification will approve. A system that separately evaluates the identity provenance of the new contact — even on a clean domain — will escalate.
